Approach 1: Advanced Energy Industries Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today’s value.

For Advanced Energy Industries, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $151.2 million, and analysts provide explicit estimates out to 2027, with projected Free Cash Flow of $226.5 million by 2027. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extrapolates ten year Free Cash Flow projections, reaching $376.3 million in 2035, and discounts each year back to today using its own assumptions.

Pulling all those discounted cash flows together, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $137.73 per share. Compared with the current share price of around $369, this implies the stock is 168.0% above the DCF estimate. This points to a rich valuation on this cash flow view.

data.title: Wärtsilä Oyj Abp (HLSE:WRT1V) Net Margin Reaches 9.4% Testing Bullish Narratives
data.description:
Wärtsilä Oyj Abp (HLSE:WRT1V) opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of €1.6b and basic EPS of €0.25. This shows how its recent profit expansion is feeding through to both the top and bottom line. Over the past year, quarterly revenue has moved from €1.56b in Q1 2025 to a range of €1.63b to €2.00b, while quarterly EPS stepped from €0.21 in Q1 2025 to between roughly €0.23 and €0.32, with trailing twelve month EPS at about €1.10. With net income and margins having trended higher over the last year, this set of results keeps the focus squarely on how sustainably Wärtsilä can defend its profitability.

See our full analysis for Wärtsilä Oyj Abp.

The next question is how these numbers line up against the widely followed stories around Wärtsilä's growth, quality of earnings, and margin resilience, and where those narratives might need updating.

See what the community is saying about Wärtsilä Oyj Abp

Advertisement

Margins Backed By 9.4% Net Profit Level

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, Wärtsilä earned €650 million of net income on €6.9b of revenue, which implies a 9.4% net margin compared with 8.1% a year earlier.
  • Supporters of the bullish view argue that margin expansion can continue, and the latest numbers give them some backing, but also some checks:
    • The move in trailing net income from €540 million to €650 million over the last year lines up with the reported 20.4% earnings growth, which fits the bullish idea that profits are scaling faster than sales.
    • At the same time, the bullish narrative talks about margins reaching around 9.4% in a few years, while the trailing figure is already at 9.4%. Future progress would therefore need to come from holding or improving that level rather than catching up to it.

Strong recent margin levels and earnings growth are exactly what bullish investors point to when they say Wärtsilä could keep turning higher revenue into solid profits, but the data also shows how much is already achieved, which makes future execution important for that story to hold. 🐂 Wärtsilä Oyj Abp Bull Case

Premium P/E Of 32.5x Versus Peers

  • Wärtsilä currently trades on a trailing P/E of 32.5x, compared with about 20.3x for the wider European Machinery group and 20.5x for direct peers, while a DCF fair value of €30.38 sits below the current share price of €35.91.
  • Bears highlight this valuation gap as their key concern, and the numbers illustrate why they focus on it:
    • Earnings grew 20.4% over the last year, yet the P/E is roughly 12 points above peer and industry averages, so a lot of that growth is already reflected in the price investors are paying today.
    • The difference between the share price of €35.91 and the DCF fair value of €30.38 suggests that any disappointment against the roughly 7.1% revenue and 8.3% earnings growth forecasts could matter quickly for a valuation that already sits above the 30.99 analyst target level.

The current valuation leaves little room for mistakes in the cautious view, which is why more bearish investors focus on the premium multiples and the gap between price, DCF fair value, and the 30.99 analyst target. 🐻 Wärtsilä Oyj Abp Bear Case

TTM EPS Tops €1.10 After Multi Year Expansion

  • Trailing twelve month basic EPS has reached about €1.10, up from €0.85 two years earlier, alongside five year earnings growth that averaged 44.3% per year and the most recent 12 month increase of 20.4%.
  • Analysts with a balanced view see this EPS history as both a strength and a reference point for moderating growth expectations:
    • The shift in trailing EPS from €0.85 to €1.10 and net income from €503 million to €650 million shows how quickly profitability has scaled, but consensus now looks for earnings growth of around 8.3% a year, which is well below the 44.3% five year pace.
    • That contrast between past growth and more moderate forecasts helps explain why analysts collectively sit at a 30.99 target while the market price is €35.91. This suggests they see the recent strong EPS run as hard to repeat at the same rate.

Next Steps

data.title: Forvia Refocuses On Technology As Interiors Sale And New Chair Reshape Path
data.description:
• Forvia (ENXTPA:FRVIA) has agreed to sell its Interiors business group to Apollo, reshaping its portfolio focus.
  • The company also announced the resignation of long serving Chairman and Board Member Michel de Rosen.
  • Pierre-André de Chalendar is proposed as the new Chairman, signaling a fresh phase for Forvia's board leadership.

Forvia, a major automotive supplier, has been repositioning around technology focused areas such as electronics, clean mobility and advanced seat structures. The exit from Interiors fits into this shift, moving capital and management attention away from lower margin, more commoditized activities toward higher tech content within the vehicle.

For investors, the combination of a portfolio reshuffle and a change in the chair role means both the business mix and governance framework are evolving at the same time. It is worth watching how the Apollo transaction terms, use of proceeds and the new board leadership influence Forvia's balance sheet, investment priorities and approach to partnerships over the coming years.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Forvia by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Forvia.

📰 Beyond the headline: 1 risk and 3 things going right for Forvia that every investor should see.

The Interiors sale to Apollo, valued at €1.82b, sits at the heart of Forvia’s IGNITE plan to concentrate on technology driven areas such as electronics and clean mobility. You are seeing a classic portfolio reshaping move, with a sizeable, more commoditized activity carved out so that capital and management time can be directed toward higher content systems that often sit closer to the “brain” of the vehicle. The timing, shortly after Q1 2026 group sales of €5,135 million against €5,488 million a year earlier, underlines how important capital allocation and balance sheet strength have become for Forvia as it works through restructuring and past losses.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Forvia Narrative

  • The transaction is consistent with the existing narrative that Forvia is using divestments and portfolio streamlining to focus on higher value technology and improve earnings quality and cash generation.
  • Relying on sizable asset disposals like the Interiors sale also ties back to concerns in the narrative about execution risk around disposals and the impact on leverage if deals are delayed or terms change.
  • The combination of a large carve out and a potential new Chairman in Pierre-André de Chalendar may bring governance and oversight dimensions that are not fully reflected in the earlier narrative.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Forvia to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Execution risk around closing the €1.82b Interiors sale, including regulatory approvals, employee consultations and carve out complexity, could affect timing and balance sheet plans.
  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged that interest payments are not well covered by earnings, so if disposal proceeds or cost savings are slower than expected, financial flexibility could stay tight.
  • 🎁 The sale supports the company’s goal of concentrating on higher value added electronics and clean mobility, which could improve business mix versus peers such as Continental, Valeo and Magna.
  • 🎁 Portfolio simplification and potential deleveraging from the transaction may reduce earnings volatility over time and make future restructuring programs more manageable.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on three things: first, how close the final Interiors deal terms are to the €1.82b enterprise value and how proceeds are used, for example to cut debt or reinvest in electronics and clean mobility. Second, the pace at which Forvia’s revenue mix shifts following the sale and whether new orders in electronics and advanced seating offset the loss of Interiors sales. Third, how the board transition plays out after the June 4, 2026 AGM and whether the new chair sets clear targets around leverage, returns and capital deployment. These factors will shape how Forvia stacks up against large auto suppliers such as Continental and Magna in the next phase.

data.title: Is It Time To Reassess McKesson (MCK) After Its Strong Multi Year Share Price Run
data.description:
• Wondering if McKesson at around US$835 a share still offers value after a strong multi year run? This piece breaks down what the current price might be telling you.
  • The stock has been relatively steady in the short term, with a 0.9% decline over 7 days and a 2.9% decline over 30 days, while sitting on a 1.4% return year to date and an 18.5% return over the last year, compared with a very large gain over 5 years.
  • Recent headlines have focused on McKesson's role in the broader healthcare supply chain and ongoing sector wide interest in distribution and services companies. This backdrop helps frame why the share price has cooled slightly in the near term after a very large 3 year gain and a 343.4% return over 5 years.
  • On Simply Wall St's 6 point valuation checklist, McKesson scores 5 out of 6. The next sections will compare how traditional valuation methods line up and then finish with a more complete way to look at value that goes beyond a single model.

McKesson delivered 18.5% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Healthcare industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: McKesson Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model looks at the cash McKesson is expected to generate in the future and then discounts those cash flows back to today to estimate what the business might be worth right now.

For McKesson, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $9.61b. Analysts provide forecasts for several years, and Simply Wall St extends those estimates further out. By 2030, projected free cash flow is $7.17b, with interim annual forecasts between 2026 and 2035 ranging from about $4.65b to $9.31b before discounting.

After discounting these projected cash flows back to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $1,521.24 per share. Compared with a recent share price of around $835, this implies the stock is 45.1% undervalued according to this DCF output.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests McKesson is undervalued by 45.1%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for McKesson.

Approach 2: McKesson Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies like McKesson, the P/E ratio is a useful way to relate what you pay for each share to the earnings that share currently generates. Investors usually accept a higher or lower P/E depending on what they expect for future earnings growth and how risky they think those earnings are.

McKesson currently trades on a P/E of 23.6x. That sits slightly below the Healthcare industry average of about 24.3x and also below the peer group average of around 25.6x. On the surface, that suggests the market is putting a modestly lower earnings multiple on McKesson compared with many peers.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what P/E might be reasonable for McKesson given factors like its earnings growth profile, profit margins, industry, market cap and stock specific risks. This is more tailored than a simple comparison to industry or peers, which do not adjust for these elements. McKesson’s Fair Ratio is 33.1x, which is materially higher than the current 23.6x P/E, indicating the shares price in a lower multiple than this framework suggests.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your McKesson Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives on Simply Wall St’s Community page let you turn your view of McKesson’s story into numbers by linking your assumptions for future revenue, earnings, margins and a fair value to the current price, then updating that view when fresh news or earnings arrive. This is why one investor might build a “higher conviction” McKesson Narrative around analysts’ more optimistic assumptions and a US$1,107 price target, while another might anchor a more cautious Narrative on the lower US$860 target and focus on risks like regulation, consolidation or biosimilars, using the gap between each Narrative’s Fair Value and today’s share price to help decide whether the stock currently looks attractive, fully priced or expensive under their own story.
